description: Publish SKILL.md — a machine-readable skill file at the repo root documenting every xrpl-cli command for AI agents, including installation, security rules, flag-by-flag reference, and copy-paste workflows

userStories.title: Create SKILL.md skeleton with frontmatter
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a canonical SKILL.md at the repo root so agents automatically receive it in their context.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at SKILL.md (repo root, same level as README.md)
File starts with YAML frontmatter block containing: name: xrpl-cli, description: one-line summary, version matching package.json version field
File is not listed in .gitignore
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Installation section to SKILL.md
userStories.description: As an agent or developer, I want clear installation instructions so I can get xrpl-cli running before invoking any commands.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains an '## Installation' section as the first section after frontmatter
Documents global install: npm install -g xrpl-cli-ng
Documents zero-install alternative: npx xrpl-cli-ng <command>
Documents Node.js 22+ requirement
Includes smoke-test: xrpl --version
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Security Rules section to SKILL.md
userStories.description: As an agent, I want explicit security constraints so I never accidentally expose seed phrases or private keys.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains a '## Security Rules for Agents' section placed before any command documentation
Rule documented: Never log, echo, or store --seed / --private-key values
Rule documented: Prefer --keystore <path> --password <pass> over raw --seed in automated pipelines
Rule documented: Never commit seed values to version control
Rule documented: Rotate any seed that appears in shell history or logs
Note documented: wallet private-key output must be treated as a secret and not forwarded to downstream tools
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document global flags and root options
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to know which global flags apply to every command so I can set --node and other options correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains a '## Global Options' section
Documents --node <url|mainnet|testnet|devnet> with default value testnet
Documents --version and --help
Includes one example showing --node combined with a subcommand (e.g. xrpl --node testnet account balance <address>)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document wallet subcommands
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to create, import, and sign with wallets so I can fund test accounts and authorize transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains a '## wallet' section documenting all subcommands: new, new-mnemonic, import, list, address, public-key, private-key, sign, verify, fund, alias, change-password, decrypt-keystore, remove
Each subcommand entry has: one-sentence description, markdown flags table (columns: Flag | Type | Required | Default | Description), and one example invocation
Flags table uses markdown table syntax (| col | col |)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document account subcommands
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to query and configure XRPL accounts so I can check balances, trust lines, and account settings.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains an '## account' section documenting all subcommands: info, balance, set, delete, set-regular-key, trust-lines, offers, channels, transactions, nfts, mptokens
Each subcommand entry has: one-sentence description, flags table, and one example invocation
account delete entry includes warning: 'Permanently removes the account from the ledger; requires destination and fee reserve'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document payment, trust, offer, and clawback commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to send payments and manage trust lines and DEX offers so I can automate common XRPL value-transfer workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains '## payment' section (alias: send) documenting flags: --amount, --destination, --seed/--keystore/--mnemonic, --memo, --destination-tag, --no-wait, --dry-run, --json; includes one example
SKILL.md contains '## trust' section documenting subcommands set and delete with flags --limit, --currency, --issuer; one example per subcommand
SKILL.md contains '## offer' section documenting subcommands create and cancel with flags --taker-pays, --taker-gets, --sequence; one example per subcommand
SKILL.md contains '## clawback' section documenting flags --amount, --holder; includes one example
Each section includes --json and --dry-run flags where supported
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document channel, escrow, and check commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to manage payment channels, escrows, and checks so I can automate conditional and streamed payments.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains '## channel' section documenting subcommands: create, fund, claim, close; each with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## escrow' section documenting subcommands: create, finish, cancel; each with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## check' section documenting subcommands: create, cash, cancel; each with flags table and one example
Each section includes --json and --dry-run flags where supported
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document AMM and NFT commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to interact with AMM pools and NFTs so I can automate DeFi and digital asset workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains '## amm' section documenting subcommands: create, deposit, withdraw, vote, bid, delete, info; each with flags table and one example

amm delete entry includes note: 'Only succeeds when the AMM pool has >512 LP token holders and tfWithdrawAll returned tecINCOMPLETE; with few holders, AMMWithdraw(tfWithdrawAll) auto-deletes the pool'
SKILL.md contains '## nft' section documenting subcommands: mint, burn, offer-create, offer-accept, offer-cancel, list; each with flags table and one example
Each section includes --json and --dry-run flags where supported
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document multisig, oracle, ticket, and credential commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to use multi-signature, oracle, ticket, and credential features so I can handle governance and identity workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains '## multisig' section documenting subcommands: set, sign, submit; each with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## oracle' section documenting subcommands: set, delete; each with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## ticket' section documenting subcommand: create; with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## credential' section documenting subcommands: create, accept, delete; each with flags table and one example
Each section includes --json and --dry-run flags where supported
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document MPToken, permissioned-domain, vault, DID, and deposit-preauth commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I want to use MPT, vaults, DIDs, permissioned domains, and deposit preauth so I can work with all amendment-gated XRPL features.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains '## mptoken' section documenting subcommands: create-issuance, destroy-issuance, authorize, unauthorize, mint, burn; each with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## permissioned-domain' section documenting subcommands: create, update, delete; each with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## vault' section documenting subcommands: create, deposit, withdraw, delete, clawback; each with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## did' section documenting subcommands: set, delete; each with flags table and one example
SKILL.md contains '## deposit-preauth' section documenting subcommands: authorize, unauthorize; each with flags table and one example
Each section includes --json and --dry-run flags where supported
Total SKILL.md line count does not exceed 1500 lines
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Common Agent Workflows section
userStories.description: As an agent, I want copy-paste workflow examples so I can quickly compose multi-step operations correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md contains a '## Common Agent Workflows' section after all command documentation
Workflow 1 'Fund a new wallet and send XRP': shows full CLI sequence — wallet new, wallet fund, payment send — with placeholder values and --node testnet
Workflow 2 'Create an IOU trust line and receive tokens': shows trust set then payment send with placeholder values
Workflow 3 'Create and drain an AMM pool': shows amm create then amm withdraw with tfWithdrawAll flag
Each workflow uses realistic placeholder values (e.g. rXXX... for addresses)
Typecheck passes
